notebooks_tldr: The community's top-voted notebooks predominantly focus on hybrid inference pipelines that combine template-based modeling (TBM), Protenix diffusion model outputs, and de-novo A-form helix fallbacks to generate a fixed number of coordinate samples per RNA target. They heavily emphasize handling long sequences via overlapping chunking and Kabsch alignment, applying physics-informed adaptive RNA constraints, and using diversity transforms to ensure structural variation. The consensus prioritizes deterministic post-processing, coordinate clipping, and strategic fallback mechanisms over traditional training loops or complex loss functions.
all_preprocessing: Coordinate extraction via C1' atom masks/heuristics
Global sequence alignment for template matching
Gap filling via linear interpolation and random walk
all_augmentations: Adaptive RNA constraints (bond lengths, i-i+2 distances, Laplacian smoothing, self-avoidance)
all_ensemble_patterns: Filling fixed sample slots (5 or 10) via sequential TBM/Protenix/de-novo fallbacks
Fixed weighted averaging of top templates [0.40, 0.25, 0.15, 0.12, 0.08]
all_post_processing: Linear weight blending for seam stitching
Iterative geometric constraint refinement with decaying strength
all_what_worked: Version 4 (TBM=False, all MSA/template flags False) achieved the best leaderboard score of 0.249.
all_what_did_not_work: Version 3 (TBM=True, others False) scored 0.408, indicating TBM alone or with limited MSA was less effective than pure Protenix inference.
all_critical_findings: TBM alignment quality heavily depends on sequence identity thresholds; model collapse (identical coordinates) can occur and requires explicit detection and zero-resetting; C1' atom indexing varies between featurizers (11 vs 12), requiring a length-matching fallback heuristic.
all_notable_techniques: Template adaptation via affine transforms and coordinate interpolation
Diversity generation using hinge rotation, chain jittering, and spline wiggle
Adaptive RNA constraints enforcing bond lengths, i-i+2 distances, Laplacian smoothing, and self-avoidance
Chunked diffusion inference with Kabsch alignment and linear blending for long sequences
De-novo fallback using idealized A-form helix geometry
Hybrid TBM + diffusion inference pipeline
Physics-informed adaptive RNA constraints (bond/angle enforcement + self-avoidance)
Deterministic de-novo A-form helix fallback for low-similarity targets
Stoichiometry-aware chain segmentation mapping
Chunk-and-stitch with Kabsch alignment and linear blending for long sequences
Fallback to idealized A-form helix with fixed rise/radius for empty prediction slots
Template-to-query coordinate mapping via alignment-aware gap interpolation and random walk
Confidence-weighted adaptive geometric constraints (sequential distance, steric clash, base-pair targeting)
De novo structure generation using helical seeding and base-pair guided random walk
Gaussian noise injection scaled inversely by template similarity score
Slot-specific diversity generation (hinge rotation, chain jittering, spline wiggle)
Heuristic C1' atom masking using atom_to_tokatom_idx indices 11/12
De-novo fallback using idealized A-form RNA helix parameters (10 Å radius, 2.5 Å rise, 0.6 rad twist)
Adaptive RNA constraints enforcing ~5.95Å backbone bonds, ~10.2Å i-i+2 distances, Laplacian smoothing, and distance-based self-avoidance
Dynamic Protenix config updates per target sequence length
Template coordinate adaptation via global sequence alignment mapping
Linear interpolation and random walk for coordinate gap filling
RNA geometric constraints enforcing sequential distance (5.5-6.5 Å) and steric clash prevention (>3.8 Å)
De novo structure generation using helical initialization, base-pairing logic, and constrained random walk
Length-based ensemble fallback (TBM for >1000 nt sequences to avoid RNAPro OOM/accuracy issues)
Custom multi-chain/multicopy USalign evaluation with chain-order mapping
Global alignment with strong terminal gap penalties (-8) to prevent residue sliding
Vectorized coordinate chunk transfer using `alignment.aligned` instead of character-by-character loops
Adaptive backbone constraint strength scaled inversely by alignment confidence
Confidence-weighted noise injection for ensemble diversity (noise decreases as similarity increases)
Hybrid template/Protenix/de-novo fallback pipeline ensuring exactly 5 predictions per target
Iterative geometric constraint refinement with decaying strength across backbone, 2-step, laplacian, and long-range distance terms
Helical gap propagation (+3.8 Å along x-axis) for unaligned regions
MSA gating for Protenix based on sequence length threshold (<=400 nt)
Fixed weighted ensemble of top 5 templates [0.40, 0.25, 0.15, 0.12, 0.08]
notable_individual_insights: votes 232 (Stanford RNA 3D Folding Part 2|Protenix+TBM): TBM alignment quality heavily depends on sequence identity thresholds; model collapse (identical coordinates) can occur and requires explicit detection and zero-resetting; C1' atom indexing varies between featurizers (11 vs 12), requiring a length-matching fallback heuristic.

votes 209 (Stanford RNA 3D | TaBM Optimized | Stable LB-0.371): Global alignment with strong terminal gap penalties (-8) prevents residue sliding, and confidence-weighted noise injection (noise = max(0.006, (0.38 - sim) * 0.07)) ensures ensemble diversity inversely proportional to similarity.

votes 203 ([0.409] Stanford RNA Folding 2: Protenix+ Template): MSA gating for Protenix is critical at <=400 nt, and iterative geometric constraint refinement with decaying strength across backbone, 2-step, laplacian, and long-range terms yields more plausible structures.

votes 232 (RNAPro inference with TBM): Replacing RNAPro predictions with TBM coordinates for sequences >1000 nt avoids OOM/accuracy issues, and custom multi-chain/multicopy USalign evaluation requires explicit chain-order mapping.

votes 274 (High Score Without Hash): Heuristic C1' atom masking using atom_to_tokatom_idx indices 11/12 and de-novo fallback using idealized A-form RNA helix parameters (10 Å radius, 2.5 Å rise, 0.6 rad twist) ensures deterministic coverage for low-similarity targets.
